headline: Review & Preview: Streak Snapped
summary: The stock market overcame a steep early slide to mostly finish higher.  The S&P 500 rose 0.1%, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.2%. headline: Tesla Needs Panasonic, BYD Doesn't — That Gap May Matter More Than You Think
summary: Tesla, Inc’s latest push into Japan isn't just about service centers and Superchargers — it's a reminder of something the market may be underestimating: not all EV growth is created equal for suppliers. Because while Tesla scales, companies like Panasonic Holdings Corp scale with it. And while BYD Co., Ltd. grows even faster, Panasonic doesn't. That asymmetry is easy to miss — but hard to ignore once you see it. Tesla Growth Flows Through Panasonic Tesla still runs on a hybrid battery model. Don

headline: Tesla Sales Rebound From Last Year’s Slump
summary: After a year marked by sluggish electric-vehicle sales, Tesla’s final tally for the first quarter was 358,023 EVs delivered globally, missing analysts’ estimates of 368,900 deliveries, according to a consensus of 19 investment firms’ estimates published by Visible Alpha.  Despite the growth from the year-ago quarter, it was Tesla’s second worst quarter of sales since 2022.  The growth in deliveries comes as Tesla pivots its business away from vehicle sales to focus on robotaxis and humanoid robots, neither of which is currently for sale.

raw.headline: Tesla Sales Rebound From Last Year’s Slump

Despite the growth from the year-ago quarter, it was Tesla’s second worst quarter of sales since 2022.  The growth in deliveries comes as Tesla pivots its business away from vehicle sales to focus on robotaxis and humanoid robots, neither of which is currently for sale.

headline: Tesla's deliveries miss estimates as tax credit expiry weighs
summary: STORY: Tesla missed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter deliveries.The auto giant saw demand hit by the expiry of U.S. tax credits on the purchase of electric vehicles.It delivered just over 358,000 vehicles in the January-March period - below market expectations of close to 369,000.That was down 14.4% from the fourth quarter, but it was up 6.3% from a year earlier.Thursday's update marked the first time Tesla had posted two consecutive years of falling deliveries.Analysts have cut their forecasts for deliveries this year, and some warned of a third straight annual drop.Tesla lost its title as the world's largest EV maker last year to China's BYD.There were some green shoots in Europe, a region that weighed on Tesla's global figures last year.The company has shown signs of recovery there and gained share in key markets like France in the first quarter.Tesla's China-made EV sales also rose for a second consecutive quarter.They were up just over 23% for the January to March period - well up from a near 2% rise in the fourth quarter.But the expiry of a $7,500 federal tax credit at the end of September was a blow to U.S. EV demand.Analysts expect the credit's removal to hurt EV sales this year.They further said Tesla also faces growing competition in the region.Shares were down around 4% after the update.

raw.headline: Tesla's deliveries miss estimates as tax credit expiry weighs
raw.summary: STORY: Tesla missed Wall Street expectations for first-quarter deliveries.The auto giant saw demand hit by the expiry of U.S. tax credits on the purchase of electric vehicles.It delivered just over 358,000 vehicles in the January-March period - below market expectations of close to 369,000.That was down 14.4% from the fourth quarter, but it was up 6.3% from a year earlier.Thursday's update marked the first time Tesla had posted two consecutive years of falling deliveries.Analysts have cut their forecasts for deliveries this year, and some warned of a third straight annual drop.Tesla lost its title as the world's largest EV maker last year to China's BYD.There were some green shoots in Europe, a region that weighed on Tesla's global figures last year.The company has shown signs of recovery there and gained share in key markets like France in the first quarter.Tesla's China-made EV sales also rose for a second consecutive quarter.They were up just over 23% for the January to March period - well up from a near 2% rise in the fourth quarter.But the expiry of a $7,500 federal tax credit at the end of September was a blow to U.S. EV demand.Analysts expect the credit's removal to hurt EV sales this year.They further said Tesla also faces growing competition in the region.Shares were down around 4% after the update.

headline: Tesla sales rise after brutal year of Musk boycotts but still fall short of expectations
summary: Sales of Tesla vehicles rose in the past three months in a possible sign recovery is afoot after a brutal year of boycotts over Elon Musk's politics but still fell short of expectations.  The positive figure follows a year of plunging sales due to an aging lineup and boycotts over Musk's right-wing political stands.  The sales in the three months through March were 6% lower than the 381,000 that financial analysts had expected, according to a survey by researcher FactSet.

Coming into Thursday trading, the S&P 500 was up 3.2% for the week, leaving it down 4.4% since the fighting in Iran began.
